evals.prompt: I want to know exactly who is visiting our pricing page so my sales team can follow up. We're a US-focused B2B SaaS company. How do I set up RB2B for this?
evals.expected_output: Guide covering pixel installation, Hot Pages configuration for pricing page, Slack alert setup, person-level ID explanation with US-only limitation, and follow-up workflow recommendations

assertions.name: pixel_setup
assertions.description: Must explain how to install the RB2B pixel (JavaScript snippet in <head> tag or via GTM) and verify it's working

assertions.name: hot_pages
assertions.description: Must recommend configuring the pricing page as a Hot Page to flag high-intent visitors

assertions.name: person_level_us_only
assertions.description: Must explain that person-level identification only works for US-based visitors, with expected match rates (35-45% on Pro+)

evals.prompt: We're currently using Clearbit Reveal for website visitor identification and considering switching to RB2B. What are the key differences and is it worth the switch?
evals.expected_output: Comparison covering person-level vs company-level identification, US-only limitation, pricing differences, Clearbit's HubSpot acquisition impact, and recommendation based on use case

assertions.name: person_vs_company
assertions.description: Must clearly explain that RB2B offers person-level ID (name, email, LinkedIn) while Clearbit Reveal only provides company-level identification

assertions.name: geographic_limitation
assertions.description: Must note that RB2B person-level ID is US-only while Clearbit Reveal works globally

assertions.name: clearbit_hubspot_context
assertions.description: Must mention that Clearbit is now Breeze Intelligence under HubSpot, which affects standalone API availability and may create HubSpot lock-in

evals.prompt: I want to use the RB2B API to resolve IP addresses to LinkedIn profiles for our custom analytics platform. How does the API work and what will it cost?
evals.expected_output: Guide covering the Identity Resolution API at api.rb2b.com, IP→LinkedIn endpoint, credit-based pricing, authentication, and chaining endpoints for richer data

assertions.name: api_separate_product
assertions.description: Must explain that the RB2B API is a separate product from the pixel with its own credit-based pricing at api.rb2b.com

assertions.name: ip_linkedin_endpoint
assertions.description: Must describe the IP→LinkedIn endpoint and how to authenticate with an API key

assertions.name: credit_pricing
assertions.description: Must include credit pricing tiers (e.g., 1,000 credits for $49, 10,000 for $299) with 1 credit per lookup
